---
title: "Target Prioritisation"
subtitle: "Shortlisting high-confidence genetic engineering candidates"
---

## Overview {#sec-targets}

This chapter is the **deliverable section**—where multi-layer evidence converges into a ranked shortlist of overexpression (OE) and knockout (KO) candidates for experimental validation.

---

## What Makes a High-Confidence Candidate?

The KG scores candidates across six dimensions:

```{=html}
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">1</div><div><strong>Direction and Strength of Association</strong> — Spearman correlation of gene/protein/metabolite abundance with qP, titre, IVCD</div></div>
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">2</div><div><strong>Multi-omics Signal</strong> — Consistent DE signal at both RNA and protein level (directional concordance)</div></div>
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">3</div><div><strong>Consensus Signal</strong> — Agreement across DEG, WGCNA module membership, and MOFA Factor loading</div></div>
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">4</div><div><strong>Pathway / Functional Role</strong> — Target involved in productivity-relevant biology (secretory pathway, ER stress, metabolic flux)</div></div>
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">5</div><div><strong>Impact on Growth and Titer</strong> — In-silico GEM perturbation predicting effects on secretion with minimal growth penalty <em>(in progress)</em></div></div>
<div class="step-box"><div class="step-number">6</div><div><strong>Causal Mining Evidence</strong> — Publication-evidence score from curated genetic manipulation studies</div></div>
```

---

## Overexpression Candidates

Overexpression candidates are genes that, when expressed at higher levels, are expected to **increase** productivity.

**Criteria:**

- Upregulated in high producers in ≥ 7/10 cohorts
- Positive Spearman correlation with qP (r > 0.5)
- Positive MOFA Factor 1 weight
- Member of pro-productivity WGCNA module (e.g., M1)
- Published OE evidence showing positive productivity effect (where available)

::: {.query-box}
Identify overexpression candidates: genes upregulated in high producers across ≥7/10 cohorts, with positive MOFA Factor 1 weight and positive qP correlation > 0.5.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Rank overexpression candidates by number of supporting high-producer cohorts, then by abs(log2FC).
:::

### Example: HSPA5

<span class="evidence-badge badge-deg">DEG</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-wgcna">WGCNA</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-mofa">MOFA</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-lit">Literature</span>

| Evidence layer | Value |
|---|---|
| DEG consistency | Up in 7/10 cohorts |
| Spearman qP | r = +0.52 |
| Spearman titre | r = +0.38 |
| MOFA Factor 1 weight | +0.392 |
| WGCNA module | M1 (kME = 0.660); ER processing / UPR enriched |
| TF regulators | NFE2L2 (NRF2), FOXO1, PPARA, FOXA1 |
| Literature | PMIDs 32624757, 24081924, 1373378 — GRP78/BiP OE → ↑ titre, ↑ qP |
| **Recommendation** | ▲ **OVEREXPRESS** |

---

## Knockout Candidates

Knockout candidates are genes that, when eliminated, are expected to **relieve** a productivity bottleneck.

**Criteria:**

- Downregulated in high producers in ≥ 7/10 cohorts (or upregulated, reflecting a gene that suppresses productivity)
- Negative Spearman correlation with qP (r < −0.5)
- Negative MOFA Factor 1 weight
- Member of anti-productivity WGCNA module (e.g., M18)
- Published KO evidence showing positive productivity effect (where available)

::: {.query-box}
Identify knockout candidates: genes with negative MOFA Factor 1 weight, downregulated in ≥7/10 high-producer cohorts, and negative Spearman qP correlation.
:::

### Example: ST14

<span class="evidence-badge badge-deg">DEG</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-wgcna">WGCNA</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-mofa">MOFA</span> <span class="evidence-badge badge-lit">Literature</span>

| Evidence layer | Value |
|---|---|
| DEG consistency | Down in 10/10 cohorts |
| Spearman qP | r = −0.51 |
| Spearman titre | r = −0.51 |
| MOFA Factor 1 weight | −0.303 (anti-productivity loading) |
| WGCNA module | M18 (kME = 0.595); Cell cycle / DNA replication enriched |
| TF regulators | E2F4 (padj = 3.7×10⁻¹²²), FOXM1, E2F1, MYC |
| Literature | PMID 29777593 — ST14 KO → ↓ fragmentation, ↑ product purity |
| **Recommendation** | ▼ **KNOCKOUT** |

---

## Multi-Omics Concordance Filter

Apply concordance filtering to further refine candidates:

::: {.query-box}
From the 63-gene shortlist, which have concordant RNA–protein regulation (mRNA up and protein up for OE candidates, or mRNA down and protein down for KO candidates)?
:::

---

## Molecule-Specific vs. Shared Targets

::: {.query-box}
Compare overexpression candidates between Nivo and C144. Which targets are shared across both molecules, and which are molecule-specific?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Show knockout candidates that appear in both Nivo and C144 high-vs-low cohorts.
:::

Shared targets are preferred for **cell-line-wide** engineering; molecule-specific targets may be relevant for product-specific quality or expression challenges.

---

## Exporting the Shortlist

::: {.query-box}
Export the top 20 overexpression candidates as a ranked table with columns: gene symbol, DEG cohort count, Spearman qP, MOFA Factor 1 weight, WGCNA module, literature PMID.
:::

The exported table provides a ready-to-use input for experimental planning and prioritisation discussions.
